original_user_request: Create a 3D Crossy Road game in a single HTML file using Three.js from CDN
precise_rewrite: Develop a 3D Crossy Road game that can be played entirely within a single HTML file, utilizing the Three.js library hosted via a Content Delivery Network (CDN)
assumptions: The user desires a fully self-contained solution without external dependencies beyond the CDN
Performance and efficiency are important factors in the development process

tradeoffs.name: Speed vs Quality
tradeoffs.rationale: Balancing the time required to develop the game with its overall quality and polish

tradeoffs.name: Realism vs Fun
tradeoffs.rationale: Deciding whether to prioritize a more realistic 3D environment or a more engaging gameplay experience

tradeoffs.name: Security vs Cost
tradeoffs.rationale: Evaluating the trade-off between ensuring the game's security and minimizing development costs

speed.rationale: The user likely wants a quick solution to start playing the game

quality.rationale: While speed is important, maintaining a good level of quality ensures a better user experience

realism.rationale: Realism is less critical than fun and quality in this context

fun.rationale: The primary goal is to create an enjoyable game experience for the user

security.rationale: Security is a concern, but it's not as critical as speed and fun in this scenario

cost.rationale: Cost should be considered to ensure the project remains feasible within budget constraints

definition_of_done: The game must be playable within a single HTML file
Three.js library must be sourced from a CDN
Game must include basic Crossy Road mechanics (player movement, obstacles, scoring)
Game should be visually appealing and engaging
non_goals: Incorporating advanced physics or complex AI for the game's environment
Developing a multiplayer version of the game
Extensive testing or optimization beyond basic functionality
conflict_rules: If speed and quality conflict, prioritize quality to ensure a better user experience
If realism and fun conflict, prioritize fun to maintain engagement
If security and cost conflict, prioritize security to protect user data
